title: Author re-identification lint v0 + reviewer checklist
context: Re-identification review is a named binding release gate (PLAN section 7/8): an automated lint plus a human reviewer sign-off must pass before any public release. This task authors the lint v0 and the reviewer checklist, and wires CI to block release if the checklist is unsigned. Reviewed by a qualified (sarcoma/clinical-genomics) reviewer. Binding cancer guardrails (PLAN section 7): open-access/aggregate/de-identified metadata only; no controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA) or patient-identifiable data; COSMIC/OncoKB reference-only (not ingested); provenance on every assertion; links only, no omics/sequence/image payloads re-hosted; no medical advice. No partner/requestor secured yet, so verifiedNeed is false.
objective: Author a re-identification lint v0 that flags re-id risks plus a human reviewer checklist, with CI blocking release when the checklist is unsigned.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated lint flags small-cell-count attributes, free-text leakage, identifying combinations.
Human reviewer checklist authored; sign-off recorded at reid-review/<release>.md.
CI blocks release if checklist unsigned. Reviewed by a qualified (sarcoma/clinical-genomics) reviewer.
output: A merged PR adding the re-identification lint v0, the reviewer checklist, and a reid-review/<release>.md sign-off template, with CI gating.
